intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.inventory_reconciliation: The biome has suffered a near-total collapse. Only a single, struggling Pothos cutting remains in the black pot. The white rabbit anchor is absent in the final frames.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil; no biological activity detected.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil; no biological activity detected.
plant_audit.P3: Single leaf specimen remaining; shows signs of chlorosis and severe turgor loss.
plant_audit.P4: Integrated into P2/P3 pot; no viable growth observed.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears desiccated and hydrophobic in early frames; later frames show complete lack of moisture.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3; electronic component (IC chip) present on desk surface.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_T-2: Gradual decline of leaf turgor in P3.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Rapid loss of light levels and visible plant mass.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_current: Total environmental darkness; plant specimen is barely discernible.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal neglect. The lack of light and moisture has led to the death of the majority of the specimens. The remaining P3 fragment is likely non-viable.
anomalies: Presence of an electronic integrated circuit (IC) on the desk surface.
Eggshell fragments used as soil amendment/top dressing.
Disappearance of the white rabbit scale anchor.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the indoor botanical environment. Over the observed period, the plants have succumbed to what appears to be severe dehydration and light starvation. The transition from a visible, albeit struggling, collection to a dark, empty, and lifeless desk surface is complete. The presence of non-botanical debris (IC chip) suggests the desk has been repurposed or neglected in favor of non-biological activities.
